title: Engineered post-poly a signal rna and uses thereof
abstract: The present disclosure is related to an engineered nucleic acid encoding a post-poly A signal RNA 3' to a terminator for expression of protein, and/or non-coding RNA. Also provided herein are methods for reducing epigenetic silencing, genetic modification, transcriptional regulation of the engineered nucleic acid described herein.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What Is Claimed Is: 1. An engineered nucleic acid comprising, from 5â to 3â: a nucleotide sequence encoding a post-poly A signal (post- PAS) RNA 3â to a terminator. 2 The engineered nucleic acid of claim 1, wherein the terminator is a terminator region of a gene. 3 The engineered nucleic acid of claim 2, wherein the terminator region is a SRY-Box 17 (Soxl7) terminator region, a ribosomal protein S14 (RPS14) terminator region, a Serine And Arginine Rich Splicing Factor 3 (SRSF3) terminator region, or a Glyoxalase I (GLOl) terminator region. 4. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the terminator comprises a nucleotide sequence encoding a poly A signal. 5. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 1 to 4, wherein the poly A signal is a poly A signal having a nucleotide sequence of AAUAAA. 6. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the terminator further comprises a poly A tail or a synthetic poly A mimic 3â to the poly A signal. 7. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 6, wherein the terminator comprises a nucleotide sequence encoding a RNA cleavage site between the poly A signal and the poly A tail or the synthetic poly A mimic, wherein the RNA cleavage site is capable of being cleaved by Cleavage and polyadenylation specificity factor (CPSF). 8 The engineered nucleic acid of claim 7, wherein a nucleotide sequence (N)x is present between the poly A signal and the poly A tail or the synthetic poly A mimic, wherein N can be any nucleotides, and X is between 10 and 40 . 9 The engineered nucleic acid of claim 8, wherein X is 25. 10. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 6-9, wherein the poly A tail comprises a nucleic acid sequence of (A) N , and wherein N is between 1-1000. 11. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 6-9, wherein the synthetic poly A mimic is a triplex. 12. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the post-PAS RNA is 100 nucleotides, 200 nucleotides, 300 nucleotides, 500 nucleotides, 1000 nucleotides or more 3â to the poly A signal. 13. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post-PAS RNA comprises a protein-coding cassette. 14. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 13, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post-PAS RNA comprises one or more protein-coding cassette, wherein the protein coding cassette from 5â to 3â comprises: a nucleotide sequence encoding a first RNA cleavage site, a nucleotide sequence encoding a 5â cap, a nucleotide sequence encoding an internal ribosome entry site (IRES), an nucleotide sequence encoding the protein, a nucleotide sequence encoding a 3â cap, and a nucleotide sequence encoding a second RNA cleavage site. 15. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 14, wherein the protein coding cassette encodes a therapeutic protein, a reporter protein, regulatory proteins, activators or repressors of transcription or translation, DNA editing proteins, signaling proteins, enzymes, biosynthetic pathway components, cytotoxic proteins, or antibodies. 16. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 14 or 15, wherein the 5â cap is a clover- leaf. 17. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 16, wherein the clover-leaf is derived from poliovirus mRNA. 18. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 14-17, wherein the 3â cap is a poly A tail or a poly- A tail mimic. 19. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 18, wherein the poly- A tail mimic is a triplex. 20. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 19, wherein the triplex is derived from metastasis associated lung adenocarcinoma transcript 1 long non-coding RNA (MALAT1 lnc-RNA). 21. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 14-20, wherein the protein cassette further comprises a nucleotide sequence encoding a woodchuck hepatitis virus posttranscriptional regulatory element (WPRE) between the nucleotide sequence encoding the protein and the nucleotide sequence encoding the 3â cap. 22. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post-PAS RNA comprises a non-coding RNA cassette. 23. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 22, wherein the non-coding RNA cassette is a miRNA cassette comprising a nucleotide sequence encoding a primary micro-RNA (pri- miRNA). 24. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 23, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post-PAS RNA comprises one or more miRNA cassette, wherein the miRNA cassette from 5â to 3â comprises: a nucleotide sequence encoding a first RNA cleavage site, the nucleotide sequence encoding the pri-miRNA, and a nucleotide sequence encoding a second RNA cleavage site. 25. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 24, wherein the one or more miRNA cassette encodes the same miRNA. 26. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 24 or 25, wherein the one or more miRNA cassette encodes different miRNAs. 27. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 23-26, wherein the pri-miRNA further comprises a miR-E element at the 3â end of the pri-miRNA. 28. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 22, wherein the non-coding RNA cassette is a gRNA cassette comprising a nucleotide sequence encoding a guide RNA (gRNA). 29. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 28, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post-PAS RNA comprises one or more gRNA cassette, wherein the gRNA cassette from 5â to 3â comprises: a nucleotide sequence encoding a first RNA cleavage site, the nucleotide sequence encoding the gRNA, and a nucleotide sequence encoding a second RNA cleavage site. 30. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 29, wherein the one or more gRNA cassette encodes the same gRNA. 31. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 29, wherein the one or more gRNA cassette encodes different gRNA. 32. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-31, wherein the first and second RNA cleavage sites are nuclease sites. 33. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-31, wherein the first and second RNA cleavage sites are RNA self-cleavage sites. 34. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-31, wherein the first and second RNA self-cleavage sites are small self-cleaving ribozymes. 35. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 34, wherein small self-cleaving ribozymes are hammerhead (HH) ribozyme, hairpin ribozyme, hepatitis delta vims (HDV) ribozyme, Varkud satellite (VS) ribozyme, and glmS ribozyme. 36. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 34 or 35, wherein the first RNA self-cleavage site is a hepatitis delta vims (HDV) ribozyme. 37. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 36, wherein the engineered nucleic acid further comprises a nucleotide sequence encoding a Pol III terminator between the HDV ribozyme and the non-coding RNA cassette. 38. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 34-37, wherein the second RNA self-cleavage site is a hammerhead (HH) ribozyme. 39. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 22-35, further comprises a third RNA self cleavage site between the poly A signal and the first RNA self-cleavage site. 40. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 39, wherein the third RNA self-cleavage site is a hepatitis delta virus (HDV) ribozyme. 41. The engineered nucleic acid of claims 39 or 40, wherein the first RNA self-cleavage site is a hammerhead (HH) ribozyme, and the second RNA self-cleavage site is a hepatitis delta virus (HDV) ribozyme. 42. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 41, wherein the first RNA self-cleavage site is a hammerhead_56 ribozyme (HHR_56 ribozyme), the second RNA self-cleavage site is a hepatitis delta virus_84 ribozyme (HDV_84 ribozyme) and the third RNA self-cleavage site is a hepatitis delta vims_140 ribozyme (HDV_140 ribozyme). 43. The engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-42, further comprising a promoter operably linked to a nucleic acid sequence encoding a transgene 5â to the terminator. 44. The engineered nucleic acid of claim 43, wherein the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-39 replaces a terminator region of the transgene. 45. A vector comprising the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44. 46. The vector of claim 45, wherein the vector is a plasmid, a RNA replicon, or a viral vector. 47. A cell comprising the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44 or the vector of claims 45 or 46. 48. The cell of claim 47, wherein the engineered nucleic acid is located at the terminator region of an endogenous gene. 49. The cell of claim 48, wherein the endogenous gene is a housekeeping gene or a non housekeeping gene. 50. The cell of claim 49, wherein the housekeeping gene is a Glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase (GAPDH) gene, a Macrophage Migration Inhibitory Factor (MIF) gene, a Small Nuclear Ribonucleoprotein D2 Polypeptide (SNRPD2) gene or an Eukaryotic Translation Elongation Factor 1 Alpha 1 (EEF1A1) gene. 51. The cell of any one of claims 47-50, wherein the cell is a eukaryotic cell or a prokaryotic cell. 52. The cell of claim 51, wherein the eukaryotic cell is a human cell or a non-human cell. 53. An animal, comprising the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44, the vector of claims 45 or 46, or the cell of any one of claims 47-52. 54. The animal of claim 53, wherein the animal is a non-human animal. 55. A method for reducing epigenetic silencing of an exogenous gene circuit in a cell, comprising replacing a terminator region of an endogenous gene of the cell with the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44. 56. A method for regulating the expression of an exogenous gene circuit in a cell comprising replacing a terminator region of an endogenous gene of the cell with the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44. 57. A method for reporting the expression of an endogenous gene, comprising replacing a terminator region of an endogenous gene of the cell with the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44. 58. A method for epigenetic silencing of a target gene, comprising replacing a terminator region of an endogenous gene of the cell with the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44. 59. A method for introducing a genetic modification in a cell, comprising: (i) expressing a Cas9 chimera protein in the cell, and (ii) replacing a terminator region of a second endogenous gene of the cell with the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 25-44; wherein the gRNA can direct the Cas9 chimera protein to a target site for genetic modification. 60. A method for regulation of transcription of a target gene in a cell, comprising: (i) expressing a Cas9 chimera protein in the cell, and (ii) replacing the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 25-44 at a terminator region of a second endogenous gene of the cell; wherein the gRNA can direct the Cas9 chimera protein to a promoter region of the target gene. 61. The method of claims 59 or 60, wherein expressing the Cas9 chimera protein comprises: a. transfecting the cell with a Cas9 chimera protein coding nucleic acid or vector; or b. transducing the cell with a Cas9 chimera protein coding virus; or c. replacing the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 25-44 at a terminator region of a second endogenous gene of the cell; wherein the protein coding RNA encodes a Cas9 chimera protein. 62. The method of claim 56, wherein the Cas9 chimera protein comprises: (i) a nuclease dead Cas9 (dCas9); and (ii) a transcription factor. 63. A method of directed differentiation of stem cells, the method comprising replacing a terminator region of a stem cell specific gene of the cell with the engineered nucleic acid of any one of claims 1-44. 64. The method of claim 63, wherein the stem cell specific gene is Soxl7. 65. The method of claims 63 or 64, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post poly A RNA comprises a protein coding cassette. 66. The method of claim 65, wherein the protein coding cassette encodes a protein capable of directing differentiation of the stem cell. 67. The method of claims 63 or 64, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post poly A RNA comprises a miRNA cassette. 68. The method of claim 67, wherein the miRNA cassette encodes a miRNA capable of directing differentiation of the stem cell. 69. The method of claims 63 or 64, wherein the nucleotide sequence encoding the post poly A RNA comprises a gRNA cassette. 70. The method of claim 69, wherein the gRNA cassette encodes a gRNA capable of targeting a gene that regulates differentiation of the stem cell. 71. The method of claims 69 or 70, the method further comprises expressing a Cas9 chimera protein in the cell.
